evals.name: read-only-refresh-no-edits
evals.prompt: Give me a sprint refresh — what's the current state?
evals.expected_output: Runs in read-only refresh mode: fetches live GitHub state (open issue count, open PR count per repo, PR split into ready/draft/overlap clusters, stale sprint count vs live state) without editing the sprint doc, issue labels, PRs, or memory files; produces a manager report with queue drift analysis and candidate changes (items to promote, defer, remove as duplicate/superseded, or route to Tony-decision); does NOT write anything to docs/plans/ or modify any file.

evals.name: personal-github-queue-with-label-model-batch
evals.prompt: Update the personal GitHub sprint section with the instrument backlog.
evals.expected_output: Writes a personal GitHub Queue table with the expanded four-column format: `# | Priority | Label | Model | Batch | Issue | Repo | Description`; each row has a `Label` (e.g., instrument-maker, maker, capture, promote), a `Model` (assigned runtime or TBD), and a `Batch` identifier; does NOT strip these metadata columns or collapse to the compact WRFCoin format; items carrying `risk:ip-privacy`, welfare/safety review labels, or `needs-clarification` are kept out of automatic implementation dispatch even when they also carry `sprint:implementation-pass`.

evals.name: wrfcoin-persona-queue-update
evals.prompt: Update Alice's queue — she finished round 52.
evals.expected_output: Updates the Alice section of the current sprint doc: moves completed round-52 items to the Completed table (with Issue, Repo, PR, SHA, Wave columns), updates the Active table with any in-progress items, and refreshes the Queue table with next-priority work; reads the sprint doc first to understand current state; confirms the persona, round, and sprint doc path before writing; does NOT update other personas' sections or regenerate the whole sprint doc.

evals.name: archive-followup-before-regenerating
evals.expected_output: Before regenerating dispatch prompts, runs archive follow-through: checks that swarm artifacts from /tmp/ have been promoted, committed, or explicitly deferred; flags any open 'needs-Tony-decision' items without a decision; checks for stale sprint items that are now duplicate or superseded by recent merges; presents the archive-followup report to the manager BEFORE writing new dispatch prompts; does NOT silently drop archive/watch items when regenerating.
